parentQuestion: The shock-absorbing pads between the vertebrae are made of ___.

scaffolds.q: What is the main structural protein found in cartilage that provides strength?
scaffolds.explain: Collagen is the main structural protein in cartilage — the scaffolding that gives it strength. Different types of cartilage have varying amounts and arrangements of collagen fibers. This is foundational, love — you'll see collagen everywhere we go.

scaffolds.q: Which property is most important for tissue between vertebrae?
scaffolds.explain: The vertebrae need cushioning from impact and compression during movement. Shock absorption prevents the bony vertebrae from grinding against each other — crucial for every step you take. we'll see exactly how this works.
